title: PhiDelay Shared-Recurrent LM (3.23 bpb, 6.84MB)
body:
## Summary

- **Track:** Non-record, unlimited compute, 16 MB artifact cap
- **val_bpb:** 3.2306 (int8+zlib roundtrip: 3.2306)
- **Artifact size:** 6.84 MB (43% of 16 MB budget)
- **Author:** Bahman Masarrat (@bahman2017)

## Key Idea

Replaces self-attention with a lightweight multi-head delay mixer (PhiDelayLayer) and introduces **recurrent weight sharing**: 3 physical transformer blocks applied 4 times each (effective depth 12). Learned per-block recurrence gates stabilize repeated application.

This work is conceptually inspired by PhiDelayNet ([DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.16790526](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16790526)), which explores adaptive delay operators for time-series forecasting. Here, those ideas are adapted for parameter-efficient language modeling under strict artifact constraints.

## Architecture

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Model dimension | 1024 |
| Physical blocks | 3 (shared) |
| Repeats | 4 |
| Effective depth | 12 |
| Total parameters | 16.8M |
| Mixer | PhiDelayLayer (multi-head, gated) |
| MLP | relu-squared, mult=2 |
| Vocab | 1024 (SentencePiece) |
| Embeddings | Tied |

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| val_bpb | **3.2306** |
| int8+zlib roundtrip val_bpb | **3.2306** |
| Artifact size | **6.84 MB** |
| Compression ratio | 3.86x |

## Key Findings

1. Training duration was the dominant bottleneck — the largest BPB gain came from more steps, not architecture changes.
2. Parameter reuse via recurrence outperformed aggressive quantized training (BitLinear: 6.09 bpb vs shared-recurrent: 3.23 bpb).
3. The int8+zlib roundtrip is essentially lossless (0.0000 bpb degradation).

## Submission Files

- `records/track_non_record_16mb/2026-03-18_PhiDelay_Shared_Recurrent_SP1024/README.md`
- `records/track_non_record_16mb/2026-03-18_PhiDelay_Shared_Recurrent_SP1024/submission.json`
- `train_gpt_mlx.py` (modified training script)
- `bitlinear.py` (present but disabled)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
